prompt: Optional is used as a field, causing serialization issues. What is the bug?
choices: Optional cannot hold String values in a Serializable class
Optional is not Serializable; using it as a field in a Serializable class causes NotSerializableException at runtime; store the raw value (String name) and expose Optional via a getter instead
Serializable classes must override readObject/writeObject when using Optional fields
Optional fields must be declared transient to avoid serialization errors
explanation: Optional does not implement Serializable (by design — it is meant for return types, not persistent storage). Using it as a field in a Serializable class causes NotSerializableException. Fix: store String name (nullable), and expose Optional<String> getName() { return Optional.ofNullable(name); } as a method.
